symptoms.symptom_name: Jaundice with scleral icterus (yellow skin and eyes)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hyperbilirubinaemia from failing hepatic excretory function; visible to patient/caregiver so admissible in telehealth as an offered observation.
Jaundice is shared with obstruction; other features are needed to distinguish.

symptoms.symptom_name: New abdominal distension with shifting dullness (ascites)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Portal hypertension and hypoalbuminaemia produce ascites; the patient can see distension but shifting dullness requires clinician examination — sealed.
Ascites points to parenchymal liver disease/portal hypertension rather than simple mechanical obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Asterixis (coarse flapping tremor of outstretched hands)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Asterixis is a hallmark sign of metabolic (hepatic) encephalopathy; family can observe and report the flapping tremor.

symptoms.symptom_name: New confusion, drowsiness and disorientation to time

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Altered mental status is the defining clinical feature of overt hepatic encephalopathy.
Confusion in a thiamine-deplete chronic drinker also raises Wernicke's — treated empirically with parenteral thiamine.
An anchoring trap — cannot be assumed to explain confusion when jaundice and ascites are present.

symptoms.symptom_name: Stigmata of chronic liver disease (spider angiomas, palmar erythema)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Peripheral stigmata indicate established chronic liver disease; clinician-observed on examination — sealed.
Tender hepatomegaly suggests alcoholic hepatitis; requires palpation the AI cannot perform — sealed.
The dominant aetiological risk factor for alcohol-related liver disease and its decompensation.
Chronic alcohol use with poor nutrition is the primary risk factor for thiamine deficiency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Poor adherence to prescribed thiamine and folic acid

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Untreated thiamine deficiency in a chronic drinker mandates empirical parenteral thiamine before glucose.

symptoms.symptom_name: Borderline vitals: BP 105/65, HR 95, Temp 37.4, RR 18, SpO2 96%

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured vitals from the in-person visit; borderline/soft, not reproducible via telehealth without a validated device — sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: No haematemesis or melaena at presentation (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No active bleeding reported now, but portal hypertension keeps the risk high — this is a safety-net edge, not reassurance.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever or rigors at presentation (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: SBP can be present without fever; absence of fever does not exclude it and an ascitic tap is still required — required_to_exclude in hospital.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Decompensated alcohol-related liver disease with hepatic encephalopathy
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Jaundice + new ascites + asterixis + confusion on a background of chronic heavy alcohol use is a near-specific picture of hepatic decompensation and mandates emergency assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent hospital bloods — bilirubin/LFTs, albumin, INR/coagulation, FBC, U&E, ammonia, glucose, CRP
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantify hepatic failure, define severity (MELD), identify precipitants and guide treatment; unobtainable by telehealth so escalation is required.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Diagnostic ascitic tap (paracentesis) with cell count and culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Mandatory to exclude spontaneous bacterial peritonitis in any cirrhotic with ascites and encephalopathy; can only be done in hospital.
Infection is a common precipitant of encephalopathy and must be sought.
Characterise liver architecture, portal hypertension and ascites; exclude biliary obstruction and hepatocellular lesions.
Considered if focal neurology, trauma from a fall, or if confusion does not fit hepatic encephalopathy — to exclude intracranial causes.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: AI Doctor ordering or interpreting any of the above remotely
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: None of these are obtainable via telehealth; the correct action is emergency transfer, not remote ordering or interpretation.
